sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Terminology 40

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Terminology 40:1
items.html:
(to the question) Zayin Zemanim de Nukva [seven times of the Nukva]
The first is from the time of Nekudim before the correction, when she was a point under the Yesod; then the power of judgment [Din] was in her, and she had not yet received the sweetenings of the quality of mercy. Therefore her place is discerned according to the degree of emanation of the ten Sefirot of Ohr Yashar [Direct Light], which is under the Yesod of ZA. The second time is at the time of the first Ibur [conception] of the correction of ZA, when she is included in the middle line in the two measures, which are Tifferet and Yesod, by the power of the ascent of NHY to CHaGaT; the three of them — Tifferet, Yesod, and Malchut — are found included in one another, and there the Nukva was completed in the measure of the Yesod. The third is in the Yenika [suckling] of ZA, when the NHY spread out from within the CHaGaT — for through the Yenika, ZA attained the discernment of the Masach [screen] of Bechina Aleph, which are the vessels of CHaGaT — and then the Nukva acquired the measure of Tifferet, in the secret of the ten points [Nekudot], meaning the Aviut [thickness] of the Masach of Bechina Aleph, which raises Ohr Chozer [Returning Light] at the level of CHaGaT; by the power of this Ohr Chozer, the ten Sefirot of the level of CHaGaT clothe in the Partzuf of ZA. Hence the level of the Nukva, too, is discerned as equal to his, since it is measured by her Ohr Chozer. However, from the respect of the Nukva in herself, there is here only the discernment of the points of the level of Tifferet — that is, the discernment of the Masach with the Aviut in it, which are called points. The fourth is in the Gadlut [greatness] of ZA, when the Nukva is rooted in his Daat; that is, the Masach and Aviut in him raise Ohr Chozer up to the discernment of the ten Sefirot of the first three of Neshama, where the discernment of CHaBaD is; then the Nukva receives the discernment of the Masach of Bechina Bet and serves in the Yesod of the Rosh, called Daat. Now in these four times, no correction has yet reached the Partzuf of the Nukva herself, for all these four times were the growth of the Nukva that is in the body of ZA, who at every degree is found at a level equal to him; she is the discernment of the Masach and Aviut and every discernment of the Ohr Chozer in the degree — all of which is called by the name BON — while ZA is the discernment of the level itself of Ohr Yashar in the degree, all of which is called by the name MA. These MA and BON are in truth literally one body, for no degree can be revealed without both of them. Hence this is not at all the discernment of the actual Nukva; rather, the Nukva afterwards receives all her roots from the discernment of the Nukva in the body of ZA, as is written in its place. Therefore we also count the ascents of the Nukva in the body of ZA as discernments of the roots of corrections for the separated Nukva of ZA — that is, her own degree, from what she was there as a distinct level before herself at the time of Nekudim.
